hero.title: mimo2codex
hero.subtitle: Let the latest OpenAI Codex CLI / desktop talk to **any** modern LLM — locally.
hero.caption: One-line install · Local proxy · ASCII-branded boot banner

features.title: Why mimo2codex
features.subtitle: Built for engineers who want Codex without OpenAI lock-in.

codex.title: Native Codex compatibility
codex.body: On-the-fly translation between Responses API and Chat Completions — no client changes.

providers.title: Multi-provider routing
providers.body: MiMo · DeepSeek · Kimi · MiniMax · SenseNova, plus any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

thinking.title: Thinking-mode control
thinking.body: `disableThinking` and forced-high-effort knobs, fine-grained per provider and per model.

docker.title: Docker · multi-user
docker.body: Built-in auth and bootstrap-URL flow ready for shared-instance production deployments.

websearch.title: Built-in web_search
websearch.body: Native MiMo web_search with auto-fallback when the upstream cannot handle it.

mimoskill.title: mimoskill extensions
mimoskill.body: OCR · image generation · pet generation — fills capability gaps the upstream lacks.

showcase.title: A polished admin console
showcase.subtitle: Visibility into every byte that crosses the proxy.

dashboard.heading: Real-time observability
dashboard.lead: The Dashboard surfaces request volume, error rate, token usage, cache-hit rate, P50 latency, and live Token-burn trends — all in one screen.

points.0: Total requests · error count · token totals · cache-hit · P50 latency
points.1: Hourly / daily token-burn chart with prompt + cache + completion layers
points.2: Enabled-provider count and error-code distribution at a glance

logs.heading: Drill into every request
logs.lead: Chat logs let you inspect client-model vs upstream-model, expand into structured view or raw JSON, and audit every tool call.

points.0: Side-by-side Client model / Upstream model columns reveal rewrites
points.1: Structured and Raw-JSON expansions per row
points.2: Tool list (function / web_search / custom / namespace) shown inline
points.3: CSV export · provider/model filters · old-log cleanup

quickstart.title: Three ways to deploy
quickstart.subtitle: Power users on the CLI, teams on Docker, non-technical users on the desktop app — pick what fits.

cli.title: Command line (CLI)
cli.body: Global install via npm — single binary, zero runtime config required.

docker.title: Docker deployment
docker.body: For intranet / team setups — user login, BYOK, OAuth, downloadable Codex config bundles.

desktop.title: Windows / macOS desktop app
desktop.body: Download and run — background service, starts on boot, no terminal required.

providers.title: Works with what you already have
providers.subtitle: Click any provider for a step-by-step setup guide.
providers.extra: + Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — see the generic-providers guide

cta.title: Ready to switch your stack?
cta.subtitle: Free, open-source, and runs entirely on your machine. Your keys, your data.
